title: taraxacum baeckiiforme
intro: Taraxacum baeckiiforme, a lesser-known species within the Taraxacum genus, is a type of dandelion plant native to East Asia. It is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, and is traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant's roots and leaves have been used in herbal preparations to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and support liver function, making it a valuable remedy for various health benefits. In traditional medicine, Taraxacum baeckiiforme is often consumed as a tea or used in decoctions, and its extracts are also used in modern herbal supplements.
